source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And Jesus went thence, and departed into the coasts of Tyre and Sidon.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And, behold, a woman of Canaan came out of the same coasts, and cried unto Him,saying, Have mercy on me, O Lord, Thou Son of David; my daughter is grievously vexed with a devil.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But Mark saith, that “He could not be hid,”though He had entered into the house.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why did He go at all into these parts?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When He had set them free from the observance of meats, then to the Gentiles also He goes on to open a door, proceeding in due course; even as Peter, having been first directed to annul this law, is sent to Cornelius.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if any one should say, “How then, while saying to His disciples, “Go not into the way of the Gentiles,”doth He Himself admit her?” first, this would be our reply, that what He enjoined upon His disciples, He was not Himself also tied to; secondly, that not in order to preach did He depart; which indeed Mark likewise intimating said, He even hid Himself, yet was not concealed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as His not hastening to them first was a part of the regular course of His proceedings, so to drive them away when coming to Him was unworthy of His love to man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if the flying ought to be pursued, much more ought the pursuing not to be avoided.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See at any rate how worthy this woman is of every benefit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For she durst not even come to Jerusalem, fearing, and accounting herself unworthy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For were it not for this, she would have come there, as is evident both from her present earnestness, and from her coming out of her own coasts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And some also taking it as an allegory say, that when Christ came out of Judea, then the church ventured to approach Him, coming out herself also from her own coasts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is said, “Forget thine own people and thy father’s house.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For both Christ went out of His borders, and the woman out of her borders, and so it became possible for them to fall in with each other: thus He saith, “Behold a woman of Canaan coming out of her own coasts.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The evangelist speaks against the woman, that he may show forth her marvellous act, and celebrate her praise the more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when thou hearest of a Canaanitish woman, thou shouldest call to mind those wicked nations, who overset from their foundations the very laws of nature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And being reminded of these, consider also the power of Christ’s advent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they who were cast out, that they might not pervert any Jews, these appeared so much better disposed than the Jews, as even to come out of their coasts, and approach Christ; while those were driving Him away, even on His coming unto them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having then come unto Him, she saith nothing else, but “Have mercy on me,” and by her cry brings about them many spectators.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed it was a pitiful spectacle to see a woman crying aloud in so great affliction, and that woman a mother, and entreating for a daughter, and for a daughter in such evil case: she not even venturing to bring into the Master’s sight her that was possessed, but leaving her to lie at home, and herself making the entreaty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And she tells her affliction only, and adds nothing more; neither doth she drag the physician to her house, like that nobleman, saying, “Come and lay thy hand upon her,” and, “Come down ere my child die.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But having described both her calamity, and the intensity of the disease, she pleads the Lord’s mercy, and cries aloud; and she saith not, “Have mercy on my daughter,” but, “Have mercy on me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For she indeed is insensible of her disease, but it is I that suffer her innumerable woes; my disease is with consciousness, my madness with perception of itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But He answered her not a word.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What is this new and strange thing? the Jews in their perverseness He leads on, and blaspheming He entreats them, and tempting Him He dismisses them not; but to her, running unto Him, and entreating, and beseeching Him, to her who had been educated neither in the law, nor in the prophets, and was exhibiting so great reverence; to her He doth not vouchsafe so much as an answer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whom would not this have offended, seeing the facts so opposite to the report?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whereas they had heard, that He went about the villages healing, her, when she had come to Him, He utterly repels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And who would not have been moved by her affliction, and by the supplication she made for her daughter in such evil case?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not as one worthy, nor as demanding a due, not so did she approach Him, but she entreated that she might find mercy, and merely gave a lamentable account of her own affliction; yet is she not counted worthy of so much as an answer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Perhaps many of the hearers were offended, but she was not offended.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why say I, of the hearers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I suppose that even the very disciples must have been in some degree affected at the woman’s affliction, and have been greatly troubled, and out of heart.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nevertheless not even in this trouble did they venture to say, “Grant her this favor,” but, “His disciples came and besought Him, saying, Send her away, for she crieth after us.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For we too, when we wish to persuade any one, oftentimes say the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But Christ saith, “I am not sent, but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then did the woman, after she heard this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Was she silent, and did she desist? or did she relax her earnestness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By no means, but she was the more instant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But it is not so with us; rather, when we fail to obtain, we desist; whereas it ought to make us the more urgent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet, who would not have been driven to perplexity by the word which was then spoken?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why His silence were enough to drive her to despair, but His answer did so very much more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For together with herself, to see them also in utter perplexity that were pleading with her, and to hear that the thing is even impossible to be done, was enough to cast her into unspeakable perplexity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet nevertheless the woman was not perplexed, but on seeing her advocates prevail nothing, she made herself shameless with a goodly shamelessness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whereas before this she had not ventured so much as to come in sight (for “she crieth,” it is said, “after us”), when one might expect that she should rather depart further off in utter despair, at that very time she comes nearer, and worships, saying, “Lord, help me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What is this, O woman?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hast thou then greater confidence than the apostles? more abundant strength?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Confidence and strength,” saith she, “by no means; nay, I am even full of shame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet nevertheless my very shamelessness do I put forward for entreaty; He will respect my confidence.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Heardest thou not Him saying, “I am not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “I heard,” saith she, “but He Himself is Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore neither did she say, “Entreat and beseech,” but, “Help me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not even with all this was He satisfied, but He makes her perplexity yet more intense again, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “It is not meet to take the children’s bread and to cast it to the dogs.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when He vouchsafed her a word, then He smote her more sharply than by His silence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And no longer doth He refer the cause to another, nor say, “I am not sent,” but the more urgent she makes her entreaty, so much the more doth He also urge His denial.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He calls them no longer “sheep,” but “children,” and her “a dog.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith the woman?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Out of His own very words she frames her plea.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Why, though I be a dog,” said she, “I am not an alien.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Justly did Christ say, “For judgment am I come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The woman practises high self-command, and shows forth all endurance and faith, and this, receiving insult; but they, courted and honored, requite it with the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “that food is necessary for the children,” saith she, “I also know; yet neither am I forbidden, being a dog.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For were it unlawful to receive, neither would it be lawful to partake of the crumbs; but if, though in scanty measure, they ought to be partakers, neither am I forbidden, though I be a dog; nay, rather on this ground am I most surely a partaker, if I am a dog.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With this intent did Christ put her off, for He knew she would say this; for this did He deny the grant, that He might exhibit her high self-command.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if He had not meant to give, neither would He have given afterwards, nor would He have stopped her mouth again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But as He doth in the case of the centurion, saying, “I will come and heal him,”that we might learn the godly fear of that man, and might hear him say, “I am not worthy that Thou shouldest come under my roof;”and as He doth in the case of her that had the issue of blood, saying, “I perceive that virtue hath gone out of me,”that He might make her faith manifest; and as in the case of the Samaritan woman, that He might show how not even upon reproof she desists:so also here, He would not that so great virtue in the woman should be hid.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not in insult then were His words spoken, but calling her forth, and revealing the treasure laid up in her.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But do thou, I pray thee, together with her faith see also her humility.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He had called the Jews “children,” but she was not satisfied with this, but even called them “masters;” so far was she from grieving at the praises of others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Why, the dogs also,”saith she, “eat of the crumbs that fall from their master’s table.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou the woman’s wisdom, how she did not venture so much as to say a word against it, nor was stung by other men’s praises, nor was indignant at the reproach?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou her constancy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He said, “It is not meet,” and she said, “Truth, Lord;” He called them “children,” but she “masters;” He used the name of a dog, but she added also the dog’s act.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou this woman’s humility?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear the proud language of the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “We be Abraham’s seed, and were never in bondage to any man;”and, “We be born of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not so this woman, rather she calls herself a dog, and them masters; so for this she became a child.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “O woman, great is thy faith.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, therefore did He put her off, that He might proclaim aloud this saying, that He might crown the woman.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Be it unto thee even as thou wilt.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now what He saith is like this: “Thy faith indeed is able to effect even greater things than these; nevertheless, Be it unto thee even as thou wilt.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This was akin to that voice that said, “Let the Heaven be, and it was.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And her daughter was made whole from that very hour.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how this woman too contributed not a little to the healing of her daughter?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to this purpose neither did Christ say, “Let thy little daughter be made whole,” but, “Great is thy faith, be it unto thee even as thou wilt;” to teach thee that the words were not used at random, nor were they flattering words, but great was the power of her faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The certain test, however, and demonstration thereof, He left to the issue of events.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Her daughter accordingly was straightway healed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark thou, I pray thee, how when the apostles had failed, and had not succeeded, this woman had success.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So great a thing is assiduity in prayer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, He had even rather be solicited by us, guilty as we are, for those who belong to us, than by others in our behalf.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet they had more liberty to speak; but she exhibited much endurance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And by the issue He also excused Himself to His disciples for the delay, and showed that with reason He had not assented to their request.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And Jesus departed from thence, and came nigh unto the sea of Galilee; and went up into the mountain, and sat down there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And great multitudes came unto Him, having with them those that were lame, blind, maimed, dumb; and cast themat His feet; and He healed them, insomuch that the multitudes wondered, when they saw the dumb to speak, the maimed to be whole, the lame to walk, and the blind to see, and they glorified the God of Israel.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now He goes about Himself, now sits awaiting the diseased, and hath the lame brought up unto the mountain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And no longer do they touch so much as His garment, but advance a higher step, being cast at His feet: and they showed their faith doubly, first, by going up into the mountain though lame, then by wanting nothing else but to be cast at His feet only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And great was the marvel and strange, to see them that were carried walking, the blind needing not any to lead them by the hand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, both the multitude of the healed, and the facility of their cure amazed them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou, how the woman indeed He healed with so much delay, but these immediately? not because these are better than she is, but because she is more faithful than they.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, while in her case He defers and delays, to manifest her constancy; on these He bestows the gift immediately, stopping the mouths of the unbelieving Jews, and cutting away from them every plea.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the greater favors one hath received, so much the more is he liable to punishment, if he be insensible, and the very honor make him no better.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore you see the rich also proving wicked, are more punished than the poor, for not being softened even by their prosperity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For tell me not that they gave alms.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since if they gave not in proportion to their substance, not even so shall they escape; our alms being judged not by the measure of our gifts, but by the largenessof our mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if these suffer punishment, much more they that are eager about unnecessary things; who build houses of two and three stories, but despise the hungry; who give heed to covetousness, but neglect alms-giving.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But since the discourse hath fallen on almsgiving, come then, let us resume again to-day that argument, which I was making three days ago concerning benevolence, and left unfinished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Ye remember, when lately I was speaking of vanity about your shoes, and of that empty trouble, and the luxury of the young, that it was from almsgiving that our discourse passed on to those charges against you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What were the matters then at that time brought forward?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That almsgiving is a kind of art, having its workshop in Heaven, and for its teacher, not man, but God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then inquiring what is an art, and what not an art, we came upon fruitless labors, and evil devices, amongst which we made mention also of this art concerning men’s shoes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Have ye then recalled it to mind?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Come now, let us to-day also resume what we then said, and let us show how almsgiving is an art, and better than all arts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if the peculiarity of art is to issue in something useful, and nothing is more useful than almsgiving, very evidently this is both an art, and better than all arts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it makes for us not shoes, nor doth it weave garments, nor build houses that are of clay; but it procures life everlasting, and snatches us from the hands of death, and in either life shows us glorious, and builds the mansions that are in Heaven, and those eternal tabernacles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This suffers not our lamps to go out, nor that we should appear at the marriage having filthy garments, but washes them, and renders them purer than snow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For though your sins be as scarlet, I will make them white as snow.”not send them away fasting, lest they faint in the way.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Both above, when going to do this miracle, He first healed them that were maimed in body, and here He doth the self-same thing; from the healing of the blind and the lame, He goes on to this again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But why might it be, that then His disciples said, “Send away the multitude,” but now they said not so; and this, though three days had past?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Either being themselves improved by this time, or seeing that the people had no great sense of hunger; for they were glorifying God for the things that were done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But see how in this instance too He doth not proceed at once to the miracle, but calls them forth thereunto.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the multitudes indeed who had come out for healing durst not ask for the loaves; but He, the benevolent and provident one, gives even to them that ask not, and saith unto His disciples, “I have compassion, and will not send them away fasting.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For lest they should say that they came having provisions for the way, He saith, “They continue with me now three days;” so that even if they came having any, it is all spent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For therefore He Himself did not this on the first and second day, but when all had been consumed by them, in order that having first been in want, they might more eagerly accept His work.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He saith, “Lest they faint in the way;” implying both their distance to be great, and that they had nothing left.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then, if thou art not willing to send them away fasting, wherefore dost thou not work the miracle?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That by this question and by their answer He might make the disciples more heedful, and that they might show forth their faith, coming unto Him, and saying, “Make loaves.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not even so did they understand the motive of His question; wherefore afterwards He saith to them, as Mark relates, “Are your hearts so hardened?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having eyes, see ye not? and having ears, hear ye not?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since, if this were not so, wherefore doth He speak to the disciples, and signify the multitude’s worthiness to receive a benefit, and add also the pity He Himself feels?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But Matthew saith, that after this He also rebuked them, saying, “O ye of little faith, do ye not yet understand, nor remember the five loaves of the five thousand, and how many baskets ye took up? nor the seven loaves of the four thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So completely do the evangelists harmonize one with another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then say the disciples?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Still they creep on the ground, although He had done so very many things in order that that miracle might be kept in memory; as by His question, and by the answer, and by making them minister herein, and by distributing the baskets; but their state of mind was yet rather imperfect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also they say to Him, “Whence should we have so many loaves in the wilderness?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Both before this, and now, they make mention of the wilderness; themselves in a weak way of argument so speaking, yet even hereby putting the miracle above suspicion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, lest any should affirm (as I have indeed already said), that they obtained it from some neighboring village, the place is acknowledged, that the miracle may be believed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With this view, both the former miracle and this He works in a wilderness, at a great distance from the villages.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The disciples, considering none of all this, said, “Whence should we have so many loaves in a wilderness?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they thought verily He had said it as purposing next to enjoin them to feed the people; most foolishly; since with this intent He had said, and that lately, “Give ye them to eat,”that He might bring them to an urgent need of entreating Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But now He saith not this, “Give ye them to eat,” but what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “I have compassion on them, and will not send them away fasting;” bringing the disciples nearer, and provoking them more, and granting them clearer sight, to ask these things of Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in truth they were the words of one signifying that He hath power not to send them away fasting; of one manifesting His authority.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the expression, “I will not,” implies such a purpose in Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since however they still spake of the multitude merely, and the place, and the wilderness (for “whence,” it is said, “should we have in a wilderness so many loaves, as to fill so great a multitude”?); and not even so understood what He said, He proceeds to contribute His own part, and saith unto them,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “How many loaves have ye?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they say, Seven, and a few little fishes.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they no more say, “But what are these among so many?”as they had said before.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that although they reached not His whole meaning, yet nevertheless they became higher by degrees.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so He too, arousing their mind hereby, puts the question much as He had done before, that by the very form of the inquiry He might remind them of the works already done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But as thou hast seen their imperfection hereby, so do thou observe the severity of their spirit, and admire their love of truth, how, writing themselves, they conceal not their own defects, great as they were.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it was no small blame to have presently forgotten this miracle, which had so recently taken place; wherefore they are also rebuked.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And herewith consider also their strictness in another matter, how they were conquerors of their appetite; how disciplined to make little account of their diet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For being in the wilderness and abiding there three days, they had seven loaves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now all the rest He doth as on the former occasion; thus He both makes them sit down on the ground, and He makes the loaves multiply themselves in the hands of the disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “He commanded,” it is said, “the multitude to sit down on the ground.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He took the seven loaves, and the fishes, and gave thanks, and brake, and gave to His disciples, and the disciples to the multitude.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when we come to the end, there is a difference.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “they did all eat,” so it is said, “and were filled, and they took up of the broken meat that was left,seven baskets full.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they that did eat were four thousand men, besides women and children.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But why at the former time, when there were five thousand, did twelve baskets full remain over and above, whereas here, when there were four thousand, it was seven baskets full?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what purpose, I say, and by what cause, were the remnants less, the guests not being so many?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Either then one may say this, that the baskets on this last occasionwere greater than those used before,or if this were not so, lest the equality of the miracle should again cast them into forgetfulness, He rouses their recollection by the difference, that by the variation they might be reminded of both one and the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Accordingly, in that case, He makes the baskets full of fragments equal in number to His disciples, in this, the other baskets equal to the loaves; indicating even hereby His unspeakable power, and the ease wherewith He exercised His authority, in that it was possible for Him to work such miracles, both in this way and in the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither was it of small power, to maintain the exact number, both then and now; then when there were five thousand, now when there were four thousand; and not suffer the remnants to be more than the baskets used on the one occasion or on the other, although the number of the guests was different.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the end again was like the former.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as then He left the multitude and withdrew in a ship, so also now; and John also saith this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since no sign did so work upon them to follow Him, as the miracle of the loaves; and they were minded not only to follow Him, but also to make Him a king;avoiding all suspicion of usurping royalty, He hastens away after this work of wonder: and He doth not even go away afoot, lest they should follow Him, but by entering into a ship.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And He sent away the multitudes,” so it saith, “and went on board the ship, and came into the coasts of Magdala.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And the Pharisees and Sadducees came anddesired Him to show them a sign from Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He saith, When it is evening, ye say, Fair weather, for the sky is red; and in the morning, Foul weather today, for the sky is red and lowering.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Ye can discern the face of the sky, but can ye not the signs of the times?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: A wicked and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign, and there shall no sign be given unto it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He left them, and departed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But Mark saith, that when they were come unto Him, and were questioning with Him, “He sighed deeply in His spirit, and saith, Why doth this generation seek after a sign?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet surely their inquiry was deserving of anger and great displeasure; yet nevertheless the benevolent and provident One is not angry, but pities and bewails them as incurably diseased, and after so full a demonstration of His power, tempting Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not in order to believe did they seek, but to lay hold of Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since had they come unto Him as ready to believe, He would have given it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He who said to the woman, “It is not meet,”and afterwards gave, much more would He have shown His bounty to these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But since they did not seek to believe, therefore He also calls them hypocrites, because in another place they said one thing, and meant another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, had they believed, they would not even have asked.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And from another thing too it is evident that they believed not; that when reproved and exposed, they abode not with Him, nor said, “We are ignorant and seek to learn.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But for what sign from Heaven were they asking?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Either that He should say the sun, or curb the moon, or bring down thunderbolts, or work a change in the air, or some other such thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith He to all this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Ye can discern the face of the sky, but can ye not discern the signs of the times?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See His meekness and moderation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not even as before did He refuse merely, and say, “There shall none be given them,” but He states also the cause why He gives it not, even though they were not asking for information.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then was the cause?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Much as in the sky,” saith He, “one thing is a sign of a storm, another of fair weather, and no one when he saw the sign of foul weather would seek for a calm, neither in calm and fair weather for a storm; so should you reckon with regard to me also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this present time of my coming, is different from that which is to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now there is need of these signs which are on the earth, but those in Heaven are stored up against that time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now as a physician am I come, then I shall be here as a judge; now to seek that which is gone astray, then to demand an account.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore in a hidden manner am I come, but then with much publicity, folding up the heaven, hiding the sun, not suffering the moon to give her light.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then ‘the very powers of the heavens shall be shaken,’and the manifestation of my coming shall imitate lightning that appears at once to all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not now is the time for these signs; for I am come to die, and to suffer all extremities.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Heard ye not the prophet, saying, “He shall not strive nor cry, neither shall His voice be heard without?”and another again, “He shall come down as rain upon a fleece of wool?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if men speak of the signs in Pharaoh’s time, there was an enemy then from whom deliverance was needed, and it all took place in due course.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But to Him that came among friends there was no need of those signs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And besides, how shall I give the great signs, when the little are not believed?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Little, I mean, as regards display, since in power these latter were much greater than the former.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what could be equal to remitting sins, and raising the dead, and driving away devils, and creating a body, and ordering all other things aright?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But do thou see their hardened heart, how on being told, that “no sign should be given them but the sign of the prophet Jonas,” they do not ask.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet, knowing both the prophet, and all that befell him, and having been told this a second time, they ought to have inquired and learnt what the saying could mean; but, as I said, there is no desire of information in these their doings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause “He also left them, and departed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And when His disciples,” so it is said, “were come to the other side, they forgot to take bread.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then Jesus said unto them, Take heed and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why said He not plainly, Beware of their teaching?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: His will is to remind them of what had been done, for He knew they had forgotten.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But for accusing them at once there seemed to be no reasonable ground, but to take the occasion from themselves, and so to reprove them, would make the charge admissible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And why did He not then reprove them, when they said, ‘Whence should we have so many loaves in the wilderness?’ for it seemed a good time then to say what He says here.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That He might not seem to rush hastily on the miracle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And besides, He would not blame them before the multitude, nor seek honor in their presence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And now too the accusation had greater reason, for that after repetition of the miracle they were so minded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He works another miracle, and then and not till then He reproves; I mean, He brings forward what they were reasoning in their hearts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what were their reasonings?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Because,” so it is said, “we have taken no bread.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as yet they were full of trepidation about the purifications of the Jews, and the observances of meats.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore on all accounts He attacks them even with severity, saying, “Why reason ye in yourselves, O ye of little faith, because ye have brought no bread?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Perceive ye not yet, neither understand?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Have ye your heart hardened?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having eyes, see ye not?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having ears, hear ye not?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do ye not remember the five loaves of the five thousand, and how many baskets ye took up? neither the seven loaves of the four thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou intense displeasure?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For nowhere else doth He appear to have so rebuked them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore then doth He so?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In order again to cast out their prejudice about the meats.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I mean that with this view, whereas then He had only said, “Perceive ye not, neither understand?” in this place, and with a strong rebuke, He saith, “O ye of little faith.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not everywhere is lenity a good thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as He used to allow them freedom of speech, so doth He also reprove, by this variety providing for their salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And mark thou at once His reproof, how strong, and His mildness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For all but excusing Himself to them for His severe reproofs to them, He saith, “Do ye not yet consider the five loaves, and how many baskets ye took up; and the seven loaves, and how many baskets ye took up?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to this end He sets down also the numbers, as well of the persons fed as of the fragments, at once both bringing them to recollection of the past, and making them more attentive to the future.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to teach thee how great the power of His reproof, and how it roused up their slumbering mind, hear what saith the evangelist.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For Jesus having said no more, but having reproved them, and added this only, “How is it that ye do not understand, that I spake it not to you concerning bread that ye should beware, but of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees;” He subjoined, saying, “Then understood they that He bade them not beware of the leaven of bread, but of the doctrine of the Pharisees and Sadducees,”although He had not uttered that interpretation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See how much good His reproof wrought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it both led them away from the Jewish observances, and when they were remiss, made them more heedful, and delivered them from want of faith;

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore hath he mentioned the founder of the city?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because there was another besides, Cæsarea Stratonis.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not in that, but in this doth He ask them, leading them far away from the Jews, so that being freed from all alarm, they might speak with boldness all that was in their mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore did He not ask them at once their own opinion, but that of the people?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In order that when they had told the people’s opinion, and then were asked, “But whomsay ye that I am?” by the manner of His inquiry they might be led up to a sublimer notion, and not fall into the same low view as the multitude.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Accordingly He asks them not at all in the beginning of His preaching, but when He had done many miracles, and had discoursed with them of many and high doctrines, and had afforded so many clear proofs of His Godhead, and of His unanimity with the Father, then He puts this question to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He said not, “Whom say the Scribes and Pharisees that I am?” often as these had come unto Him, and discoursed with Him; but, “Whom do men say that I am?” inquiring after the judgment of the people, as unbiassed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though it was far meaner than it should be, yet was it free from malice, but the other was teeming with much wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And signifying how earnestly He desires His Economyto be confessed, He saith, “The Son of Man;” thereby denoting His Godhead, which He doth also in many other places.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He saith, “No man hath ascended up to Heaven, but the Son of Man, which is in Heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again, “But when ye shall see the Son of Man ascend up, where He was before.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, since they said, “Some John the Baptist, some Elias, some Jeremias, or one of the prophets,”and set forth their mistaken opinion, He next added, “But whom say ye that I am?”calling them on by His second inquiry to entertain some higher imagination concerning Him, and indicating that their former judgment falls exceedingly short of His dignity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He seeks for another judgment from themselves, and puts a second question, that they might not fall in with the multitude, who, because they saw His miracles greater than human, accounted Him a man indeed, but one that had appeared after a resurrection, as Herod also said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He, to lead them away from this notion, saith, “But whom say ye that I am?” that is, “ye that are with me always, and see me working miracles, and have yourselves done many mighty works by me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith the mouth of the apos tles, Peter, the ever fervent, the leader of the apostolic choir?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When all are asked, he answers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whereas when He asked the opinion of the people, all replied to the question; when He asked their own, Peter springs forward, and anticipates them, and saith, “Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Blessed art thou, Simon Barjona, for flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet surely unless he had rightly confessed Him, as begotten of the very Father Himself, this were no work of revelation; had he accounted our Lord to be one of the many, his saying was not worthy of a blessing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since before this also they said, “Truly He is Son of God,”those, I mean, who were in the vessel after the tempest, which they saw, and were not blessed, although of course they spake truly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they confessed not such a Sonship as Peter, but accounted Him to be truly Son as one of the many, and though peculiarly so beyond the many, yet not of the same substance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Nathanael too said, “Rabbi, Thou art the Son of God, Thou art the King of Israel;”and so far from being blessed, he is even reproved by Him, as having said what was far short of the truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He replied at least, “Because I said unto thee, I saw thee under the fig-tree, believest thou? thou shalt see greater things than these.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why then is this man blessed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because he acknowledged Him very Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore you see, that while in those former instances He had said no such thing, in this case He also signifies who had revealed it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, lest his words might seem to the many (because he was an earnest lover of Christ) to be words of friendship and flattery, and of a disposition to show favor to Him, he brings forward the person who had made them ringin his soul; to inform thee that Peter indeed spake, but the Father suggested, and that thou mightest believe the saying to be no longer a human opinion, but a divine doctrine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore doth He not Himself declare it, nor say, “I am the Christ,” but by His question establish this, bringing them in to confess it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because so to do was both more suitable to Him, yea necessary at that time, and it drew them on the more to the belief of the things that were said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how the Father reveals the Son, how the Son the Father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “neither knoweth any man the Father,” saith He, “save the Son, and he to whomsoever the Son will reveal Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It cannot therefore be that one should learn the Son of any other than of the Father; neither that one should learn the Father of any other than of the Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that even hereby, their sameness of honor and of substance is manifest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Thou art Simon, the son of Jonas; thou shalt be called Cephas.”“Thus since thou hast proclaimed my Father, I too name him that begat thee;” all but saying, “As thou art son of Jonas, even so am I of my Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Else it were superfluous to say, “Thou art Son of Jonas;” but since he had said, “Son of God,” to point out that He is so Son of God, as the other son of Jonas, of the same substance with Him that begat Him, therefore He added this, “And I say unto thee, Thou art Peter, and upon this rock will I build my Church;”that is, on the faith of his confession.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hereby He signifies that many were now on the point of believing, and raises his spirit, and makes him a shepherd.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And the gates of hellshall not prevail against it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And if not against it, much more not against me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So be not troubled because thou art shortly to hear that I shall be betrayed and crucified.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then He mentions also another honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And I alsowill give thee the keys of the heavens.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what is this, “And I also will give thee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “As the Father hath given thee to know me, so will I also give thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He said not, “I will entreat the Father” (although the manifestation of His authority was great, and the largeness of the gift unspeakable), but, “I will give thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What dost Thou give? tell me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “The keys of the heavens, that whatsoever thou shalt bind on earth, shall be bound in Heaven,and whatsoever thou shalt loose on earth, shall be loosed in Heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then is it not “His to give to sit on His right hand, and on His left,”when He saith, “I will give thee”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how He, His own self, leads Peter on to high thoughts of Him, and reveals Himself, and implies that He is Son of God by these two promises?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For those things which are peculiar to God alone, (both to absolve sins, and to make the church in capable of overthrow in such assailing waves, and to exhibit a man that is a fisher more solid than any rock, while all the world is at war with him), these He promises Himself to give; as the Father, speaking to Jeremiah, said, He would make him as “a brazen pillar, and as a wall;”but him to one nation only, this man in every part of the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I would fain inquire then of those who desire to lessen the dignity of the Son, which manner of gifts were greater, those which the Father gave to Peter, or those which the Son gave him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the Father gave to Peter the revelation of the Son; but the Son gave him to sow that of the Father and that of Himself in every part of the world; and to a mortal man He entrusted the authority over all things in Heaven, giving him the keys; who extended the church to every part of the world, and declared it to be stronger than heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For heaven and earth shall pass away, but my word shall not pass away.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then is He less, who hath given such gifts, hath effected such things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And these things I say, not dividing the works of Father and Son (“for all things are made by Him, and without Him was nothing made which was made”):but bridling the shameless tongue of them that dare so to speak.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But see, throughout all, His authority: “I say unto thee, Thou art Peter; I will build the Church; I will give thee the keys of Heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And then, when He had so said, “He charged them that they should tell no man that He was the Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why did He charge them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That when the things which offend are taken out of the way, and the cross is accomplished, and the rest of His sufferings fulfilled, and when there is nothing any more to interrupt and disturb the faith of the people in Him, the right opinion concerning Him may be engraven pure and immovable in the mind of the hearers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, in truth, His power had not yet clearly shone forth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Accordingly it was His will then to be preached by them, when both the plain truth of the facts, and the power of His deeds were pleading in support of the assertions of the apostles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it was by no means the same thing to see Him in Palestine, now working miracles, and now insulted and persecuted (and especially when the very cross was presently to follow the miracles that were happening); and to behold him everywhere in the world, adored and believed, and no more suffering anything, such as He had suffered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He bids them “tell no man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that which hath been once rooted and then plucked up, would hardly, if planted, again be retained among the many; but that which, once fixed, hath remained immovable, and hath suffered injury from no quarter, easily mounts up, and advances to a greater growth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if they who had enjoyed the benefit of many miracles, and had had part in so many unutterable mysteries, were offended by the mere hearing of it; or rather not these only, but even the leaderof them all, Peter; consider what it was likely the common sort should feel, being first told that He is the Son of God, then seeing Him even crucified and spit upon, and that without knowledge of the secret of those mysteries, or participation in the gift of the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if to His disciples He said, “I have many things to say unto you, but ye cannot bear them now;”much more would the rest of the people have utterly failed, had the chiefest of these mysteries been revealed to them before the proper time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Accordingly He forbids them to tell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to instruct thee how great a thing it was, their afterwards learning His doctrine complete, when the things that offend had passed by; learn it from this same leader of theirs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this very Peter, he who after so many miracles proved so weak as even to deny Him, and to be in fear of a mean damsel; after the cross had come forth, and he had received the certain proofs of the resurrection, and there was nothing more to offend and trouble him, retained the teaching of the Spirit so immovable, that more vehemently than a lion he sprang upon the people of the Jews, for all the dangers and innumerable deaths which were threatened.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With reason then did He bid them not tell the many before the crucifixion, since not even to them that were to teach did He venture to commit all before the crucifixion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For I have many things to say unto you,” saith He, “but ye cannot bear them now.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And of the things too that He did say, they do not understand many, which He did not make plain before the crucifixion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: At least when He was risen from the dead, then and not before they knew some of His sayings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “From that time forth began He to show unto them that He must suffer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: From that time.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When He had fixed the doctrine in them; when He had brought in the beginning of the Gentiles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not even so did they understand what He said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For the saying,” it is said, “was hid from them;”and they were as in a kind of perplexity, not knowing that He must rise again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He rather dwells on the difficulties, and enlarges His discourse, that He may open their mind, and they may understand what it can be that He speaks of.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But they understood not, but the saying was hid from them, and they feared to ask this;”not whether He should die, but how, and in what manner, and what this mystery could be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they did not even know what was this same rising again, and supposed it much better not to die.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, the rest being troubled and in perplexity, Peter again, in his ardor, alone ventures to discourse of these things; and not even he openly, but when he had taken Him apart; that is, having separated himself from the rest of the disciples; and he saith, “Be it far from Thee, Lord, this shall not be unto Thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What ever is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He that obtained a revelation, he that was blessed, hath he so soon fallen away, and suffered overthrow, so as to fear His passion?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what marvel, that one who had not on these points received any revelation, should have that feeling?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, to inform thee that not of himself did he speak those other things either, see in these matters that were not revealed to him how he is confounded and overthrown, and being told ten thousand times, knows not what the saying can mean.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that He is Son of God he had learnt, but what the mystery of the cross and of the resurrection might be, was not yet manifest to him: for “the saying,” it is said, “was hid from them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou that with just cause He bade them not declare it to the rest?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if it so confounded them, who must needs be made aware of it, what would not all others have felt?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He however, to signify that He is far from coming to the passion against His will, both rebuked Peter, and called him Satan.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let them hear, as many as are ashamed of the suffering of the cross of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if the chief apostle, even before he had learnt all distinctly, was called Satan for feeling this, what excuse can they have, who after so abundant proof deny His economy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I say, when he who had been so blessed, who made such a confession, has such words addressed to him; consider what they will suffer, who after all this deny the mystery of the cross.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He said not, “Satan spake by thee,” but, “Get thee behind me, Satan.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed it was a desire of the adversary that Christ should not suffer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore with such great severity did He rebuke him, as knowing that both he and the rest are especially afraid of this, and will not easily receive it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He also reveals the thoughts of his mind, saying, “Thou savorestnot the things that be of God, but those that be of men.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what means, “Thou savorestnot the things that be of God, but those that be of men”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Peter examining the matter by human and earthly reasoning, accounted it disgraceful to Him and an unmeet thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Touching him therefore sharply, He saith, “My passion is not an unmeet thing, but thou givest this sentence with a carnal mind; whereas if thou hadst hearkened to my sayings in a godly manner, disengaging thyself from thy carnal understanding, thou wouldest know that this of all things most becometh me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thou indeed supposest that to suffer is unworthy of me; but I say unto thee, that for me not to suffer is of the devil’s mind;” by the contrary statements repressing his alarm.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus as John, accounting it unworthy of Christ to be baptized by him, was persuaded of Christ to baptize Him, He saying, “Thus it becometh us,”and this same Peter too, forbidding Him to wash his feet, by the words, “Thou hast no part with me, unless I wash thy feet;”even so here too He restrained him by the mention of the opposite, and by the severity of the reproof repressed his fear of suffering.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let no man therefore be ashamed of the honored symbols of our salvation, and of the chiefest of all good things, whereby we even live, and whereby we are; but as a crown, so let us bear about the cross of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, for by it all things are wrought, that are wrought among us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whether one is to be new-born, the cross is there; or to be nourished with that mystical food, or to be ordained, or to do anything else, everywhere our symbol of victory is present.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore both on house, and walls, and windows, and upon our forehead, and upon our mind, we inscribe it with much care.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For of the salvation wrought for us, and of our common freedom, and of the goodness of our Lord, this is the sign.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For as a sheep was He led to the slaughter.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When therefore thou signest thyself, think of the purpose of the cross, and quench anger, and all the other passions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When thou signest thyself, fill thy forehead with all courage, make thy soul free.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And ye know assuredly what are the things that give freedom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also Paul leading us there, I mean unto the freedom that beseems us, did on this wise lead us unto it, having reminded us of the cross and blood of our Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For ye are bought,” saith he, “with a price; be not ye the servants of men.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Consider, saith he, the price that hath been paid for thee, and thou wilt be a slave to no man; by the price meaning the cross.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since not merely by the fingers ought one to engrave it, but before this by the purpose of the heart with much faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if in this way thou hast marked it on thy face, none of the unclean spirits will be able to stand near thee, seeing the blade whereby he received his wound, seeing the sword which gave him his mortal stroke.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if we, on seeing the places in which the criminals are beheaded, shudder; think what the devil must endure, seeing the weapon, whereby Christ put an end to all his power, and cut off the head of the dragon.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Be not ashamed then of so great a blessing, lest Christ be ashamed of thee, when He comes with His glory, and the sign appears before Him, shining beyond the very sunbeam.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed the cross cometh then, uttering a voice by its appearance, and pleading with the whole world for our Lord, and signifying that no part hath failed of what pertained to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This sign, both in the days of our forefathers and now, hath opened doors that were shut up;this hath quenched poisonous drugs;this hath taken away the power of hemlock; this hath healed bites of venomous beasts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if it opened the gates of hell, and threw wide the archways of Heaven, and made a new entrance into Paradise, and cut away the nerves of the devil; what marvel, if it prevailed over poisonous drugs, and venomous beasts, and all other such things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This therefore do thou engrave upon thy mind, and embrace the salvation of our souls.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cross saved and converted the world, drove away error, brought back truth, made earth Heaven, fashioned men into angels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because of this, the devils are no longer terrible, but contemptible; neither is death, death, but a sleep; because of this, all that warreth against us is cast to the ground, and trodden under foot.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If any one therefore say to thee, Dost thou worship the crucified? say, with your voice all joy, and your countenance gladdened, “I do both worship Him, and will never cease to worship.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if he laugh, weep for him, because he is mad.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thank the Lord, that He hath bestowed on us such benefits, as one cannot so much as learn without His revelation from above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, this is the very reason of his laughing, that “the natural man receiveth not the things of the Spirit.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since our children too feel this, when they see any of the great and marvellous things; and if thou bring a child into the mysteries, he will laugh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now the heathen are like these children; or rather they are more imperfect even than these; wherefore also they are more wretched, in that not in an immature age, but when full grown, they have the feelings of babes; wherefore neither are they worthy of indulgence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But let us with a clear voice, shouting both loud and high, cry out and say (and should all the heathen be present, so much the more confidently), that the cross is our glory, and the sum of all our blessings, and our confidence, and all our crown.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I would that also with Paul I were able to say, “By which the world is crucified unto me, and I unto the world;”but I cannot, restrained as I am by various passions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore I admonish both you, and surely before you myself, to be crucified to the world, and to have nothing in common with the earth, but to set your love on your country above, and the glory and the good things that come from it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed we are soldiers of a heavenly King, and are clad with spiritual arms.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why then take we upon ourselves the life of traders, and mountebanks, nay rather of worms?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For where the King is, there should also the soldier be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, we are become soldiers, not of them that are far off, but of them that are near.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the earthly king indeed would not endure that all should be in the royal courts, and at his own side, but the King of the Heavens willeth all to be near His royal throne.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how, one may say, is it possible for us, being here, to stand by that throne?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because Paul too being on earth was where the seraphim, where the cherubim are; and nearer to Christ, than these the body guards to the king.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For these turn about their faces in many directions, but him nothing beguiled nor distracted, but he kept his whole mind intent upon the king.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that if we would, this is possible to us also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For were He distant from us in place, thou mightest well doubt, but if He is present everywhere, to him that strives and is in earnest He is near.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also the prophet said, “I will fear no evil, for Thou art with me;” and God Himself again, “I am a God nigh at hand, and not a God afar off.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then as our sins separate us from Him, so do our righteousnesses draw us nigh unto Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For while thou art yet speaking,” it is said, “I will say, Here I am.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What father would ever be thus obedient to his offspring?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What mother is there, so ready, and continually standing, if haply her children call her?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is not one, no father, no mother: but God stands continually waiting, if any of his servants should perchance call Him; and never, when we have called as we ought, hath He refused to hear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He saith, “While thou art yet speaking,” I do not wait for thee to finish, and I straightway hearken.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us call Him therefore, as it is His will to be called.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what is this His will?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Loose,” saith He, “every band of iniquity, unloose the twisted knots of oppressive covenants, tear in pieces every unjust contract.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Break thy bread to the hungry, and bring in the poor that are cast out to thy house.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If thou seest one naked, cover him, and them that belong to thy seed thou shalt not overlook.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then shall thy light break forth in the morning, and thine healings shall spring forth speedily, and thy righteousness shall go before thee, and the glory of the Lord shall cover thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then thou shalt call upon me, and I will give ear unto thee; whilst thou art yet speaking, I will say, Lo! here I am.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And who is able to do all this? it may be asked.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, who is unable, I pray thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For which is difficult of the things I have mentioned?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which is laborious?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which not easy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, so entirely are they not possible only, but even easy, that many have actually overshot the measure of those sayings, not only tearing in pieces unjust contracts, but even stripping themselves of all their goods; making the poor welcome not to roof and table, but even to the sweat of their body, and laboring in order to maintain them; doing good not to kinsmen only, but even to enemies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what is there at all even hard in these sayings?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
