verses.text: For neither did He say, “Pass over the mountain, go across the sea, dig through so many acres of land, abide without food, wrap thyself in sackcloth;” but, “Impart to the poor,impart of thy bread, cancel the contracts unjustly made.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What is more easy than this? tell me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But even if thou account it difficult, look, I pray thee, at the rewards also, and it shall be easy to thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For much as our emperors at the horse races heap together before the combatants crowns, and prizes, and garments, even so Christ also sets His rewards in the midst of His course, holding them out by the prophet’s words, as it were by many hands.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the emperors, although they be ten thousand times emperors, yet as being men, and the wealth which they have in a course of spending, and their munificence of exhaustion, are ambitious of making the little appear much; wherefore also they commit each thing severally into the hand of the several attendants, and so bring it forward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But our King contrariwise, having heaped all together (because He is very rich, and doeth nothing for display), He so brings it forward, and what He so reaches out is indefinitely great, and will need many hands to hold it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to make thee aware of this, examine each particular of it carefully.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then,” saith He, “shall thy light break forth in the morning.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Doth not this gift appear to thee as some one thing?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But it is not one; nay, for it hath many things in it, both prizes, and crowns, and other rewards.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And, if ye are minded, let us take it to pieces and show all its wealth, as it shall be possible for us to show it; only do not ye grow weary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And first, let us learn the meaning of “It shall break forth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He said not at all, “shall appear,” but “shall break forth;” declaring to us its quickness and plentifulness, and how exceedingly He desires our salvation, and how the good things themselves travail to come forth, and press on; and that which would check their unspeakable force shall be nought; by all which He indicates their plentifulness, and the infinity of His abundance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what is “the morning.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It means, “not after being in life’s temptations, neither after our evils have come upon us;” nay, it is quite beforehand with them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as in our fruits, we call that early, which has shown itself before its season; so also here again, declaring its rapidity, he has spoken in this way, much as above He said, “Whilst thou art yet speaking, I will say, Lo! here I am.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But of what manner of light is He speaking, and what can this light be?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not this, that is sensible; but another far better, which shows us Heaven, the angels, the archangels, the cherubim, the seraphim, the thrones, the dominions, the principalities, the powers, the whole host, the royal palaces, the tabernacles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For shouldest thou be counted worthy of this light, thou shalt both see these, and be delivered from hell, and from the venomous worm, and from the gnashing of teeth, and from the bonds that cannot be broken, and from the anguish and the affliction, from the darkness that hath no light, and from being cut asunder, and from the river of fire, and from the curse, and from the abodes of sorrow; and thou shalt depart, “where sorrow and woe are fled away,”where great is the joy, and the peace, and the love, and the pleasure, and the mirth; where is life eternal, and unspeakable glory, and inexpressible beauty; where are eternal tabernacles, and the untold glory of the King, and those good things, “which eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither have entered into the heart of man;”where is the spiritual bridechamber, and the apartments of the heavens, and the virgins that bear the bright lamps, and they who have the marriage garment; where many are the possessions of our Lord, and the storehouses of the King.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how great the rewards, and how many He hath set forth by one expression, and how He brought all together?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So also by unfolding each of the expressions that follow, we shall find our abundance great, and the ocean immense.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Shall we then still delay, I beg you; and be backward to show mercy on them that are in need?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, I entreat, but though we must throw away all, be cast into the fire, venture against the sword, leap upon daggers, suffer what you will; let us bear all easily, that we may obtain the garment of the kingdom of Heaven, and that untold glory; which may we all attain, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might, world without end.
